titleStmt.title: SII 27.250: original edition by M. D. Sampath, H. K. Narasimhamurthy, Madhav N. Katti and B. R. Gopal
No. 250. (A.R. No. 9 of 1939-40.) DONGALASĀNI, (HAMLET OF KURUGUṆṬPALLE) SIDDHAVATAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.

summary.p: Slab set up by the side of the Añjanēya temple.

The inscription records that Nallachiddarasa (Nalla-siddharasa) ruling at Nellurupaṭṭaṇa, having started out on a campaign of victory, his subordinates Mahāpradhana, Sandhivigrahi Rēvaṇa-heggade and his brother Pōtaṇa-heggede encountered Mallidēva and Sōmēśvaradēva, the chiefs of Pottapidēśa at Dongalasāni; Somēśvaradēva along with twelve other chiefs and a thousand men and dispersing several others himself fell in the encounter. His brother Pōtaṇa along with others, reported the success of the fight to his over lord Nallasiddha, installed perpetual lamps at the mahāsthānas of Mallikārjuna, Tripurāntaka, Siddhavaṭa, Aṇampura and Māhēśvara for the merit of his brother and himself. Rēvaṇa's son Kāvaṇa, the record continues, consecrated an image of Vishṇu at the frontier of Peḍanāḍu for the merit of his father and his mother Dāramambikā.The inscription is followed by the Telugu and Tamil versions of which the latter, besides mentioning the other details, adds that Sōmēśvara and Mallidēva were the sons of Vimalāditya and that the inscription was engraved during the reign of Kulōttuṅga.
The record is dated Śaka 1081, Pramādi, Māgha śu. 15, arddhōdaya which details correspond to 1160 A.D., January 25.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrīmanmahāmaṁḍaḷēśvaraṁ kāṁcīpura
varādhīśvaraṁ bhujabaḷavīra nallaciddarasadēva
cōḷamahārājaru Uttarōttarābhivriddhi pravaddhamāna
mācaṁdrarkkatāraṁbaraṁ salisuttamire nellūrapaṭṭaṇado
ḷu sukhasaṁkathā vinōdadiṁ rājyaṁgeyyuttirddu digvijayā
kāṁkṣeyiṁdettibaralu pottapidēśādhīśvararappa mallidēva
sōmēśvara dēvarasaru samasta sāmagriverasidirāṁtu
niṁdalli śrīmanmahāpradhānaṁ saṁdhivigrahi rēvaṇa heggaḍeyuṁ
taṁma pōtaṇa heggeḍeyu bhayabalavaṟaye śauryya
pratijña rūḍharāgi 1081 neya pramādi saṁvatsarada mā
gha su 15 yarddhōdayadaṁdu ḍoṁgalasāniyamū
ḍaṇa huṇiseya banada yeraḍu toṟiya saṁgama
saṁgrāma raṁgadalli vīrabhaṭaruverasi yuddhavāgalu
yalli vīrāvatāranu saṁdhivigrahi rēvaṇa heggaḍe ku
dureya nēṟi harisi mallidēvana nōḍisi sōmēśvara dēvanū
ḷagāgi haṁnibbararasugaḷaṁ sāyirāḷaṁ koṁdu pulaba
raṁ virathaṁmāḍi tāvalli vīralakṣmiyaṁ kaikoṁḍu vaikuṁṭha prā
ptarādaru pōtaṇaheggaḍe halaveṟu verasibaṁdu viraśrī
yaṁ nallaciddarasaṁgittu baḷika taṁmaṇnagaṁ tamagaṁ
vappaṁtāgi mallikārjunatripurāṁtaka siddhavaṭa Aṇaṁpu
ra mahēsvara veṁba mahāsthānaṁgaḷalli yakhaṁḍa
dīpa mēlu paṭṭaṁgaḷaṁ māḍisidanu Ā rēvaṇa heggaḍe
gaṁ dāramāṁbikegaṁ dharmmavappaṁtāgiya varasu
putra saṁdhivigrahi kāvaṇaheggaḍe paḍenāḍa vāyilū
ṟalu śrīviṣṇupratiṣṭheyaṁ māḍi hattu vrittiyaṁ
brāhmaṇarige koṭṭaru Ā caṁdrārkkastāyi yā
galu maṁgaḷa mahā śrī śrī
At the top of the slab depicted two warriors on caparisoned horses with their names within below the figures, the one on the left as sandhivigrahi ṟēvaṇa heggaḍe and the other on the sight, sandhivigrahi Potana heggaḍe.
The inscription is followed by its version in Telugu characters and this in turn by its own copy in Nagari characters which again is followed by its Tamil version written in Tamil characters.
Digital edition of SII 27.250 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
